<<no way out: the unheard story of meera>>

meera stood at the edge of the slums, her eyes filled with the same hopelessness that had defined her life for years. every day, she worked tirelessly to earn a small amount of money, but the system was against her. the local authorities ignored her cries for help, and the police would come to arrest her for nothing. she had tried everything—education, employment, even marriage—but nothing changed. the only thing that remained was the same: a life of poverty and despair.

the world outside was a place of opportunity, but meera could not see it. she had no future, no hope, and no way out. the people around her were the same: they had no dreams, no aspirations, and no plan to escape the cycle of poverty. they lived in fear, in constant dread, and in a world that seemed to move in a different direction.

meera was intelligent, but she had no access to the resources she needed to succeed. the system was rigged against her, and the only way to break free was to fight against it. but the system was too strong, and the people who controlled it were too powerful. they saw her as a burden, a threat, and a failure.

the world outside was a place of possibility, but meera could not reach it. she had no future, no hope, and no way out. the people around her were the same: they had no dreams, no aspirations, and no plan to escape the cycle of poverty. they lived in fear, in constant dread, and in a world that seemed to move in a different direction.

the system was a monster, and meera was its victim. she had no power, no strength, and no future. the only thing that remained was the same: a life of poverty and despair.
